content: The hard working memory capacity limits that constrain an agent's step-by-step reasoning are not a bug to be engineered away, but a useful selection pressure that can drive state-of-the-art active learning agent memory systems to automatically curate high-value episodic memories. This occurs because capacity limits force curious active learning agents to discard only low-information, redundant memories, retaining only unexpected, high-curiosity experiences that improve future planning and generalization.
source_topics: agent working memory capacity limits reasoning
